commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=00dc522a8b2008e3d9e13623eb98815b75e9946b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes exec policy resolution to ignore model-supplied `ask`, updates the exec tool schema description, and adds a regression test for Telegram-origin allowlisted gateway exec.

PR surface: Source -1, Tests +39. Total +38 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main raises effective exec ask from `params.ask`, so `ask: "always"` can force an approval before the gateway allowlist path. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor/maintainer follow-up for real behavior proof and docs/API alignment, not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: fix #88773: [Bug]: Telegram DM exec requires approval despite allowlist + ask:off — works in webchat, not in Telegram
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram-origin natural-language exec could prompt for approval even when the effective policy was `security=allowlist, ask=off` and the command binary was allowlisted.
- Solution: Keep exec approval `ask` controlled by configured `tools.exec.*` plus host approvals, matching the existing treatment of model-supplied `security`.
- What changed: The exec tool no longer lets the model/provider-supplied `ask` tool argument strengthen the configured host ask policy; the tool schema now describes that `ask` is ignored for normal calls, and a regression covers a Telegram-origin allowlisted `gog` command with model-supplied `ask: "always"`.
- What did NOT change: Host approval files can still tighten policy, configured `tools.exec.ask` still works, approval-required diagnostics paths that construct exec tools with `ask: "always"` still request approval, and allowlist enforcement is unchanged.
- Fixes #88773

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram DM allowlisted exec parity with webchat when effective policy is `security=allowlist, ask=off`; a model/provider-supplied `ask` argument must not create `exec.approval.request` or `exec.approval.waitDecision`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway-host exec path in the issue worktree on Linux, with Telegram-origin exec defaults (`messageProvider: "telegram"`, Telegram target/account fields), a real executable `gog` fixture on PATH, and a real host approvals file containing wildcard allowlist trust for that binary.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools-agent-config.exec.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/exec-approvals.test.ts`.
- **Evidence after fix:** `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts` now executes `gog tasks add tasklist --title test` with `ask: "always"` supplied by the tool call, effective configured/host ask set to `off`, and asserts the command completes while `callGatewayTool` is never called for approval registration.
- **Observed result after fix:** The regression and related acceptance tests passed; `verification.log` records 14/14 passing for `bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts`, 34/34 passing for the gateway/agent-config exec tests, and 32/32 passing for Telegram exec approvals.
- **What was not tested:** A live Telegram Bot API DM was not exercised because this environment has no Telegram credentials; the tested path covers the Telegram-origin exec defaults and the host approval decision that caused the prompt before channel delivery would occur.

## Regression Test Plan
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A Telegram-origin exec call for an allowlisted `gog` binary completes under configured `ask: off` even when the model-supplied tool argument asks for `always`, and it does not register an exec approval request.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** It isolates the channel mismatch to exec policy resolution without needing external Telegram credentials, while still using the real host exec tool, PATH resolution, approvals file loading, allowlist evaluation, and gateway approval call boundary.

## Root Cause
- **Root cause:** `createExecTool` treated model-supplied `security` as ignored, but still merged model-supplied `ask` with the configured/host policy through `maxAsk(hostAsk, requestedAsk)`. Natural-language channel runs can receive provider-generated tool arguments, so a Telegram run could strengthen `ask` to `always` or `on-miss` even though the user and host approvals had selected `ask: off`.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The source of truth for exec approval policy is now consistently the configured `tools.exec.*` policy intersected with host approvals; model/tool-call arguments can no longer make Telegram-origin exec diverge from webchat for the same allowlisted command. Host approvals still tighten the policy, so the fix removes the unintended provider-controlled override without weakening the actual guardrail.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** The patch is deliberately small: one policy merge change, one schema description update, and one regression test. It does not add fallbacks or channel-specific bypasses.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The exec host policy resolver remains the source of truth; Telegram-specific delivery code is not changed because the bug occurs before approval delivery, when the exec tool decides whether an approval is needed.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
